Okay, so one of the most common discussion points is delegating. Okay, and so I want to say I had like an entire presentation for this and I want to say this as simple as possible, which is one, you need to delegate to elevate. Okay, so in order for you to go up, you must throw out the things that are holding you down, right, which means delegating. Why? Because you cannot pour into an empty cup, okay, whether it be yours or someone else's. One rule that we have is that if we are going to assign someone more responsibility, if we are going to add more to their plate, then we also have to decide what we are going to take off of their plate, what they are going to delegate, what we are going to automate, outsource, whatever, okay, because you cannot continue to pour, pour more onto yourself or anyone else in your team unless you're making sure that you're relieving yourself. Okay, let's keep it simple, okay, delegate what you hate and what you suck at, okay, that's the easiest way to put it. And then keep doing what you love and what you're great at, okay. This requires a level of self-awareness, but in order to come to that level, you just need to be doing what I asked you to do in the last one, which is talking about what you're good at, what your skill sets are, etc., okay. Delegate what you hate and what you suck at, keep doing what you love and what you're great at. The easiest way to put it.
